certified_cutline.description: A subject may publish as Certified only when it references a certified, fresh, owner-signed certified-archetype manifest entry whose backing stable claim still holds. A subject that has lost its archetype certification, has stale or missing support evidence, relied on an expired waiver, or whose backing stable claim narrowed below the stable cutline drops to a weaker positive class (supported, community, experimental) or to a refusal class and never inherits a stronger neighbour's publication.

certified_archetypes.title: Rust workspace self-host on the reference laptop
certified_archetypes.scope_summary: Rust self-host slice on macOS ARM64; certified for the named hardware, toolchain, reference-workspace, and workflow rows while the report stays fresh.
certified_archetypes.rationale: The certified-archetype report backs the launch-wedge claim for the macOS Rust self-host archetype and is current within its freshness window.

certified_archetypes.title: TypeScript web-app on the reference laptop
certified_archetypes.scope_summary: TypeScript web-app slice on Ubuntu GNOME Wayland; certified for the named platform-profile and workflow rows while the report stays fresh.
certified_archetypes.rationale: The compatibility report backs the TypeScript web-app archetype claim and is current within its freshness window.

certified_archetypes.title: Legacy remote-over-SSH workspace
certified_archetypes.scope_summary: Remote-over-SSH workspace; certification withdrawn pending a refreshed remote reference-workspace report.
certified_archetypes.rationale: The remote-over-SSH archetype lost its certification when its reference-workspace report aged out and was not refreshed; any subject still pointing here must narrow.

downgrade_rules.title: Certified claim without a manifest entry
downgrade_rules.rationale: A subject put forward as Certified that does not reference a certified-archetype manifest entry narrows below Certified until a manifest entry is published.

downgrade_rules.title: Certified-archetype report no longer fresh
downgrade_rules.rationale: A Certified claim cannot ride a certified-archetype report that has aged out of its freshness window; the report must be refreshed before the claim publishes.

downgrade_rules.title: Referenced archetype decertified
downgrade_rules.rationale: When the referenced certified-archetype manifest entry is decertified the subject narrows and routes users to a supported mode rather than claiming certification it no longer holds.

downgrade_rules.title: Required support evidence absent
downgrade_rules.rationale: A positive support class with no evidence on its required evidence path narrows; an empty evidence set cannot back a published support claim.

downgrade_rules.title: Support evidence no longer fresh
downgrade_rules.rationale: Support wording cannot ride evidence that has aged out of its freshness window; the published class narrows until the evidence is rerun.

downgrade_rules.title: Backing stable claim narrowed below cutline
downgrade_rules.rationale: A support class that depends on a stable claim narrows automatically when that claim drops below the stable cutline, so published support never outruns the stable line it ingests.

downgrade_rules.title: Provisional waiver expired
downgrade_rules.rationale: A class that only held because of a waiver narrows the moment the waiver expires; publication holds rather than silently widening.

downgrade_rules.title: Support claim lacks owner sign-off
downgrade_rules.rationale: No certified or supported claim publishes without a named owner sign-off; an unsigned row holds publication until the owner signs.

entries.title: Provider-aware language intelligence
entries.rationale: Editor-side semantic behavior references a fresh, owner-signed certified-archetype entry and its backing stable claim holds, so it publishes as Certified.

entries.title: Repair and rollback safety

waiver.reason: The rollback contract and correction-train evidence are current; an active waiver covers the still-pending live-hardware rollback drill while it is scheduled.

entries.rationale: Repair and rollback safety holds a Supported claim under an active, unexpired waiver covering the scheduled live-hardware drill; the waiver and its expiry are recorded so the class narrows automatically if it lapses.

entries.title: Export and offboarding support
entries.rationale: The referenced remote archetype is decertified and the backing stable claim is narrowed, so the surface publishes the not-certified-in-this-mode refusal class and routes users to a supported mode.

entries.title: Localization readiness
entries.rationale: The localization support evidence aged out of its freshness window and its backing stable claim is narrowed, so the published class drops to experimental until the evidence is rerun.

entries.title: Regulated-environment assurance

waiver.reason: The waiver that let regulated-environment assurance hold a Supported claim while sovereign-deploy evidence was gathered has expired without that evidence landing.

entries.rationale: The provisional waiver expired before sovereign-deploy evidence landed and the backing stable claim is narrowed, so the surface publishes the not-supported refusal class and publication holds.

publication.rationale: Three subjects put forward as Certified or Supported are narrowed below their claimed class for a decertified archetype, stale support evidence, an expired waiver, and a narrowed backing stable claim; their reasons fire four blocking downgrade rules, so the v1.0 support publication holds until they qualify or their claims are formally narrowed.
